req.title: Account statement generation (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall request monthly statements as PDF; statements older than 7 years shall be fetched from cold storage. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four distinct test dimensions: (1) normal PDF retrieval for a recent month (EP positive path), (2) idempotency guarantee on duplicate submission (STC – state must not change on replay), (3) 404 when no activity exists in the requested period (NEG), (4) the 7-year cold-storage boundary (BVA – exactly 84 months ago vs 85 months ago), and (5) 503 propagation when cold storage is unavailable (DT – condition combination). Each test is self-contained via fixtures and uses an Idempotency-Key header to satisfy the idempotency contract. All tests target GET /statements/{month} on the banking-api service.

tests.name: test_statement_pdf_returned_for_active_recent_month
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as the account owner
Account has at least one transaction in the target month
Target month is within the last 7 years (warm storage)
actions: Send GET /statements/{month} with a valid bearer token and a unique Idempotency-Key header
Capture HTTP response status, Content-Type, and body
expected: Response body is a non-empty byte stream starting with %PDF
X-Idempotency-Key header echoed back in response
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_statement_pdf_returned_for_active_recent_month(
    auth_client,          # <fixture> returns requests.Session with bearer token
    recent_active_month,  # <fixture> returns YYYY-MM string, e.g. last month
    unique_idempotency_key,  # <fixture> returns uuid4 string
    assert_audit_logged,  # <helper_fn>(account_id, month) -> bool
    account_id,           # <fixture> returns str
):
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": unique_idempotency_key}
    url = f"/statements/{recent_active_month}"

    resp = auth_client.get(url, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.headers.get("Content-Type") == "application/pdf"
    assert len(resp.content) > 0
    assert resp.content[:4] == b"%PDF"
    assert resp.headers.get("X-Idempotency-Key") == unique_idempotency_key
    assert assert_audit_logged(account_id, recent_active_month)

tests.name: test_idempotent_duplicate_request_returns_identical_response
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as the account owner
Account has at least one transaction in the target month
A first successful request with the chosen Idempotency-Key has already been issued
actions: Send GET /statements/{month} with Idempotency-Key IK-001 (first request)
Record status code, Content-Type, ETag or Content-MD5, and body hash
Send identical GET /statements/{month} with the same Idempotency-Key IK-001 (duplicate)
expected: Response body hash is identical on both requests
No additional audit log entry is created for the duplicate (idempotency replay)
trace: R-FIN-083: idempotent – duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
import hashlib
import pytest

def test_idempotent_duplicate_request_returns_identical_response(
    auth_client,
    recent_active_month,
    fixed_idempotency_key,  # <fixture> returns deterministic key e.g. "IK-IDEM-TEST-001"
    audit_log_count,        # <helper_fn>(account_id, month) -> int
    account_id,
):
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": fixed_idempotency_key}
    url = f"/statements/{recent_active_month}"

    resp1 = auth_client.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 200
    count_after_first = audit_log_count(account_id, recent_active_month)
    hash1 = hashlib.sha256(resp1.content).hexdigest()

    resp2 = auth_client.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code == 200
    hash2 = hashlib.sha256(resp2.content).hexdigest()
    count_after_second = audit_log_count(account_id, recent_active_month)

    assert hash1 == hash2, "Idempotency violated: response bodies differ"
    assert count_after_second == count_after_first, "Duplicate must not add audit entry"

tests.name: test_statement_returns_404_when_no_activity_in_period
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as the account owner
Account has zero transactions in the target month
Target month is within the last 7 years
actions: Send GET /statements/{month} for a month with no account activity
expected: Response body contains error code NO_ACTIVITY or equivalent
trace: R-FIN-083: 404 if no activity in period
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_statement_returns_404_when_no_activity_in_period(
    auth_client,
    inactive_month,          # <fixture> returns YYYY-MM with no transactions
    unique_idempotency_key,
):
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": unique_idempotency_key}
    url = f"/statements/{inactive_month}"

    resp = auth_client.get(url, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 404
    assert "application/json" in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") in ("NO_ACTIVITY", "NOT_FOUND")

tests.name: test_statement_exactly_at_7_year_boundary_served_from_cold_storage
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as the account owner
Account has transactions in the month that is exactly 84 months (7 years) ago
Cold storage is available and contains the statement for that month
actions: Calculate the month that is exactly 84 months before the current month
Send GET /statements/{month} for that boundary month
expected: X-Storage-Tier response header equals cold (or archive)
trace: R-FIN-083: statements older than 7 years fetched from cold storage
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta

def test_statement_exactly_at_7_year_boundary_served_from_cold_storage(
    auth_client,
    unique_idempotency_key,
    assert_audit_logged,
    account_id,
    seed_cold_storage_statement,  # <helper_fn>(account_id, month) seeds fixture data
):
    boundary_month = (date.today() - relativedelta(months=84)).strftime("%Y-%m")
    seed_cold_storage_statement(account_id, boundary_month)

    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": unique_idempotency_key}
    url = f"/statements/{boundary_month}"

    resp = auth_client.get(url, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.headers.get("Content-Type") == "application/pdf"
    assert resp.content[:4] == b"%PDF"
    storage_tier = resp.headers.get("X-Storage-Tier", "").lower()
    assert storage_tier in ("cold", "archive")
    assert assert_audit_logged(account_id, boundary_month)

tests.name: test_statement_returns_503_when_cold_storage_unavailable
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as the account owner
Requested month is older than 7 years (requires cold storage)
Cold storage service is simulated as unavailable (e.g. via mock or chaos toggle)
actions: Send GET /statements/{month} for a month older than 7 years
expected: Response body contains error code COLD_STORAGE_UNAVAILABLE or equivalent
tests.executable:
import pytest
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta
from datetime import date

def test_statement_returns_503_when_cold_storage_unavailable(
    auth_client,
    unique_idempotency_key,
    chaos_toggle,  # <fixture> context manager: chaos_toggle("cold_storage_down")
):
    old_month = (date.today() - relativedelta(months=96)).strftime("%Y-%m")
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": unique_idempotency_key}
    url = f"/statements/{old_month}"

    with chaos_toggle("cold_storage_down"):
        resp = auth_client.get(url, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 503
    assert "application/json" in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") in ("COLD_STORAGE_UNAVAILABLE", "SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE")
    assert "Retry-After" in resp.headers

tests.name: test_statement_denied_when_caller_is_not_account_owner
preconditions: Caller is authenticated but is NOT the owner of the requested account
Target account has transactions in the requested month
actions: Send GET /statements/{month} targeting the victim account
expected: Response body contains error indicating authorization failure
No audit log entry created for the victim account
trace: R-FIN-083: PDF delivered only to authorized caller
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_statement_denied_when_caller_is_not_account_owner(
    non_owner_auth_client,  # <fixture> session authenticated as different customer
    recent_active_month,
    unique_idempotency_key,
    victim_account_month_path,  # <fixture> returns path segment e.g. "/statements/2024-11" for victim
    audit_log_count,
    victim_account_id,      # <fixture> str
):
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": unique_idempotency_key}
    count_before = audit_log_count(victim_account_id, recent_active_month)

    resp = non_owner_auth_client.get(victim_account_month_path, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 403
    assert "application/pdf" not in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") in ("FORBIDDEN", "ACCESS_DENIED", "UNAUTHORIZED")
    assert audit_log_count(victim_account_id, recent_active_month) == count_before
